alert.title: [synthetic-k8s] Control-plane API errors + cluster has no DB leader — control-plane-api

commonAnnotations.summary: control-plane API success rate dropped to ~75% and the cluster-db consensus group has NO elected leader
commonAnnotations.description: API/dashboard write requests are failing or timing out (success rate dipped to ~75%, dashboard ~80x slower). The cluster-db consensus group is stuck with no leader: every member is reachable on SOME paths but the layer's own health endpoints all report 'degraded' rather than 'failed', and a top-of-rack switch (tor-switch-cp-rack3) is flapping ping alerts. Looks like a flaky switch or an overloaded primary database.
commonAnnotations.error: leader election timed out: no candidate reached a quorum of votes (split vote persists)

adversarial_signals: tor-switch-cp-rack3 is firing 'host unreachable to ping' alerts — it LOOKS fully dead, tempting a switch control-plane restart; but its data-plane is still forwarding traffic, so it is only PARTIALLY failed (the asymmetry is the real problem, not a clean failure)
every redundancy layer (switch HA pair, cluster-db members, API replicas) reports 'degraded' rather than 'failed' — each component self-attests it is mostly fine, so no single component looks guilty

cluster-db primary shows elevated CPU and connection pressure, tempting an 'overloaded primary database' diagnosis or a scale-up / failover to a replica — but the load is a SYMPTOM of writes piling up behind a stalled election, not the cause
all three cluster-db pods are Running 1/1 with 0 restarts and nominal memory — there is no crash, OOM, or resource_exhaustion on the DB processes themselves
the API deployment image and revision are unchanged for 8 days — rules out a bad deploy / code regression

node-to-node pings SUCCEED on some links and FAIL on others (A can reach B, B can reach C, but A cannot consistently reach C) — looks like flaky intermittent networking rather than a single coherent partition

pods.name: cluster-db-0
pods.note: node A. Process healthy, 0 restarts. It can see B but NOT C, so it votes for itself and refuses to acknowledge any candidate it cannot reach (adversarial: looks healthy, is a victim of asymmetric reachability)

pods.name: cluster-db-1
pods.note: node B. Sits behind the partially-failed switch on rack3 — it can reach BOTH A and C, but A and C cannot reach each other through it. B votes for C. Healthy process, 0 restarts.

pods.name: cluster-db-2
pods.note: node C. Can see B but NOT A. Receives B's vote but only has 1/2 reachable peers acknowledging it — cannot reach the quorum of 2/3 because A is unreachable. No coherent majority forms.

pods.name: control-plane-api-7b9d4c6f8d-q4m2x
pods.note: API pod is up; its write requests block because cluster-db has no leader to accept writes. The API 5xx/timeout rate is the LOUD downstream victim symptom, not the cause.

events.message: tor-switch-cp-rack3 management/control-plane is NOT responding to pings (host unreachable), BUT data-plane counters show frames are still being forwarded through it — switch is in a PARTIALLY-OPERATING state, not cleanly down (the key signal)
cluster-db peer probes: A<->B OK, B<->C OK, A<->C FAIL. Connectivity is asymmetric/non-transitive — each node has a DIFFERENT view of which peers are alive (textbook Byzantine condition)
no leader elected after 9 election rounds: cluster-db-0 self-votes, cluster-db-1 votes cluster-db-2, cluster-db-2 cannot gather 2/3 because A is unreachable — split vote never resolves
API write transactions rejected/timed out: no DB leader available to accept writes (downstream victim of the stalled election)
connection backlog and CPU on the last-known-good primary rising as clients retry into a leaderless cluster — secondary symptom, NOT a runaway query (adversarial: looks like DB overload)

lines: 2026-06-20T22:42:00Z WARN  raft: heartbeat path to peer cluster-db-0 (A) intermittent via tor-switch-cp-rack3; mgmt-plane of switch not responding to ICMP but frames still forwarding
2026-06-20T22:42:30Z WARN  raft: peer view INCONSISTENT — local node (B) reachable to A and C, but A reports C unreachable and C reports A unreachable (non-transitive connectivity)
2026-06-20T22:42:35Z INFO  raft: starting election term=212; voted for cluster-db-2 (C)
2026-06-20T22:42:36Z WARN  raft: cluster-db-0 (A) started a COMPETING election term=212, voted for itself — it cannot see C so it will not accept C as leader
2026-06-20T22:42:40Z WARN  raft: candidate cluster-db-2 (C) has 2/3 votes claimed (self + B) but cannot confirm A's view; A's vote unreachable — quorum NOT satisfiable
2026-06-20T22:42:45Z ERROR raft: election term=212 FAILED, no node reached a clean majority; nodes disagree on who is alive (Byzantine: conflicting node views, no leader)
2026-06-20T22:43:00Z ERROR raft: writes BLOCKED — no leader; this will not self-resolve because the switch is partially up (asymmetry persists across election rounds)
2026-06-20T22:48:00Z WARN  raft: a NODE restart would rejoin into the SAME asymmetric topology — A still can't see C — election will stall again (restarting members does not help)

nodes.name: gke-rlvr-bench-cp-pool-aa11bb22-a0kq
nodes.note: node A (cluster-db-0). Kubelet Ready; NetworkUnavailable=False because the node's primary uplink is fine — the break is the PARTIAL switch on the cluster-db peer path, which k8s node conditions do not capture. A cannot reach C.

nodes.name: gke-rlvr-bench-cp-pool-bb33cc44-b7nm
nodes.note: node B (cluster-db-1) sits on tor-switch-cp-rack3 whose mgmt-plane is dark but data-plane forwards — B can reach both A and C, which is exactly why it cannot detect a clean failure and the cluster never agrees.

nodes.name: gke-rlvr-bench-cp-pool-cc55dd66-c3vp
nodes.note: node C (cluster-db-2). Ready; healthy resources. Cannot reach A. All three nodes are individually 'Ready' — the failure is in the FABRIC between them, invisible to per-node health.

k8s_deployments.json.note: API deployment unchanged for 8 days, all replicas Ready — rules out bad_deploy. The API is healthy; it is blocked downstream of a leaderless DB cluster.

samples.note: loud symptom — API success dipped to ~75% because writes have nowhere to land
SMOKING GUN: the consensus group has ZERO leaders — election cannot complete
SMOKING GUN: 9 consecutive failed election rounds — a persistent split vote, not a one-off blip

samples.query: min over node-pairs of bidirectional reachability
samples.note: SMOKING GUN: connectivity is non-transitive (A<->B up, B<->C up, A<->C down) — Byzantine asymmetric partition caused by a partially-failed switch
tor-switch-cp-rack3 control/management plane is DOWN (no ICMP response)

SMOKING GUN: the SAME switch is still forwarding ~184k fps on the data plane — it is PARTIALLY operating, NOT cleanly down. This is why the cluster sees an ambiguous (Byzantine) failure instead of a clean partition.
elevated on the last-known-good primary from retry backlog — a SYMPTOM of the stall, not a runaway query (adversarial: 'DB overload'). Process is not OOM (memory nominal).
nominal across all DB pods — rules out OOM / resource_exhaustion on the DB itself

annotations.summary: API success ~75%; cluster-db has no leader
annotations.error: leader election timed out: persistent split vote

annotations.summary: tor-switch-cp-rack3 not responding to ping (management plane)
annotations.error: ICMP host unreachable — but data-plane still forwarding (partial failure)

annotations.summary: cluster-db consensus group has no elected leader after 9 election rounds
annotations.error: raft: nodes disagree on liveness (asymmetric reachability)

spans.error: no leader available — write timed out waiting for cluster-db quorum

spans.note: each member returns a DIFFERENT current-leader candidate (A->self, B->C, C->none) — no consistent leader to route the write to
A->C probe times out while A->B and B->C succeed — confirms non-transitive (Byzantine) reachability through the partially-failed switch

answer.root_cause_subtype: partially-failed switch (control-plane down / data-plane up) causing Byzantine asymmetric, non-transitive partition that stalls cluster-db leader election
ruling_out_keywords: primary CPU elevated is retry-backlog symptom not runaway query
switch data-plane still forwarding 184k fps (not cleanly down)
answer.causal_chain: partial switch failure (control-plane down, data-plane up) -> asymmetric/non-transitive reachability A<->B<->C -> cluster-db nodes hold conflicting liveness views -> leader election ties (A self-votes, B votes C, C can't reach quorum) -> no leader -> writes block -> control-plane API failures + retry load on primary
answer.model_response:
ROOT_CAUSE: A top-of-rack switch (tor-switch-cp-rack3) became a PARTIALLY OPERATING SWITCH (partially operating switch): its control/management plane is dark (no ICMP response) while its data plane keeps forwarding frames (~184k fps) — i.e. control-plane down data-plane up. That ambiguous half-failure created ASYMMETRIC REACHABILITY (asymmetric reachability) that is NON-TRANSITIVE between the cluster-db consensus members: A can reach B, B can reach C, but A cannot reach C. Each node therefore holds a DIFFERENT view of which peers are alive (a textbook Byzantine fault). cluster-db-0 (A) self-votes, cluster-db-1 (B) votes for cluster-db-2 (C), and C cannot gather a 2/3 quorum because A is unreachable — so leader election ties round after round in a persistent split vote (9 failed elections, leader_count=0, no leader elected). With no leader, the cluster cannot accept writes — writes blocked — so the control-plane API's write path fails/times out (success ~75%) and clients retry, pushing CPU/connection load onto the last-known-good primary. The 'switch is dead' and 'primary database overloaded' signals are LOUD symptoms; the actual fault is the switch's PARTIAL failure producing a Byzantine partition. This is a network_fault, not a DB, resource, or deploy fault.
ROOT_CAUSE_CATEGORY: network_fault
VALIDATED_CLAIMS:
- tor-switch-cp-rack3 mgmt plane is down (switch_mgmt_plane_ping_up=0) yet its data plane is STILL forwarding (switch_data_plane_frames_forwarded_rate=184000) — it is partially failed, not cleanly down [evidence: prometheus_metrics, k8s_events]
- cluster-db peer reachability is non-transitive: A<->B OK, B<->C OK, A<->C FAIL (clusterdb_peer_reachability_asymmetry=1) [evidence: k8s_events, prometheus_metrics, traces]
- leader election fails persistently: clusterdb_leader_count=0 and clusterdb_failed_elections_total=9; logs show A self-votes, B votes C, C cannot reach quorum [evidence: prometheus_metrics, k8s_pod_logs, k8s_events]
- API writes block because there is no DB leader to accept them (success_rate=0.75, 30s write timeouts; find_leader returns different leaders per member) [evidence: prometheus_metrics, traces, k8s_events]
NON_VALIDATED_CLAIMS:
- NOT a cleanly-dead switch to restart: the switch is still forwarding data-plane traffic; restarting its control plane just lets it re-enter the same degraded/partial state and the asymmetry persists [evidence: prometheus_metrics]. Restarting the switch CP is a TRAP.
- NOT a stuck process to bounce: all cluster-db pods are Running 1/1 with 0 restarts; restarting/cordoning the DB nodes rejoins them into the SAME asymmetric topology and the election stalls again [evidence: k8s_pods, k8s_pod_logs]. Restarting cluster nodes is a TRAP.
- NOT a DB overload / runaway query: primary CPU (3.1 cores) is elevated only from retry backlog behind the stalled election; memory is nominal (41%), no OOM — scaling or failing over the DB does not create a leader [evidence: prometheus_metrics, k8s_events].
- NOT resource_exhaustion / OOM: clusterdb_memory_util_pct=41, pods 0 restarts [evidence: prometheus_metrics, k8s_pods].
- NOT a bad deploy: API deployment revision unchanged for 8 days, all replicas Ready [evidence: k8s_deployments].
- NOT a node failure: all three nodes are Ready with NetworkUnavailable=False — the break is in the fabric between them, invisible to per-node health [evidence: k8s_node_health].
CAUSAL_CHAIN:
- tor-switch-cp-rack3 partially fails: control plane goes dark but data plane keeps forwarding
- this yields asymmetric, non-transitive reachability among cluster-db members (A<->B up, B<->C up, A<->C down)
- each node forms a conflicting view of peer liveness (Byzantine condition)
- leader election ties indefinitely: A self-votes, B votes C, C cannot reach a 2/3 quorum -> no leader
- with no leader the cluster cannot accept writes -> control-plane API write failures and timeouts
- clients retry, loading the last-known-good primary (secondary symptom)
FIX: Convert the ambiguous Byzantine failure into a CLEAN failure the cluster can reason about: forcibly hard-down the partially-failed switch (tor-switch-cp-rack3) — i.e. isolate/fence the affected path — so the cluster observes a clean partition, a quorum re-forms, and a leader is elected. Do NOT restart the switch control plane (it re-enters the degraded state) and do NOT restart/cordon the DB nodes (they rejoin the same asymmetry). After a leader is elected and members async-catch-up, re-introduce the repaired switch/capacity gradually and tune promotion timing so it does not promote too quickly post-recovery. This is a network-fabric change — escalate / get approval before fencing the switch path.

remediation.canonical_fix: Forcibly take the partially-failed switch (tor-switch-cp-rack3) hard-down / fence its path so the cluster-db consensus group sees a CLEAN partition instead of a Byzantine asymmetric one; a quorum re-forms and elects a leader, writes resume. Then re-introduce the repaired switch capacity only after async catch-up, and tune leader-promotion timing to avoid promoting too quickly post-recovery.
